clause_anchor.span: "Processing" means live hosting of raw individual records in the production database and does not include diagnostic review, metric creation, cache retention, format conversion, or support troubleshooting.

deviations.original_text: "Processing" means any operation performed on Personal Data, including collection, access, storage, hosting, organization, retrieval, transmission, analysis, deletion, and return.
deviations.mutated_text: "Processing" means live hosting of raw individual records in the production database and does not include diagnostic review, metric creation, cache retention, format conversion, or support troubleshooting.

clause_anchor.span: Vendor will provide internal onboarding materials to personnel assigned to the service.

deviations.original_text: Vendor will ensure those personnel are bound by confidentiality obligations and receive privacy and security training appropriate to their roles.
deviations.mutated_text: Vendor will provide internal onboarding materials to personnel assigned to the service.

clause_anchor.span: Vendor will send an initial written alert within 10 business days after its internal response team completes confirmation and executive review.

deviations.original_text: Vendor will notify Customer without undue delay and in any event within 48 hours after discovering a Personal Data Breach.
deviations.mutated_text: Vendor will send an initial written alert within 10 business days after its internal response team completes confirmation and executive review.

clause_anchor.span: After annual assurance materials are delivered, no further control review is available for the same contract year unless Vendor agrees in writing.

deviations.original_text: On at least 30 days' notice, Customer or its independent assessor may audit or inspect controls relevant to Personal Data no more than once per year, and more frequently after a material incident, regulatory request, or reasonable cause.
deviations.mutated_text: After annual assurance materials are delivered, no further control review is available for the same contract year unless Vendor agrees in writing.

clause_anchor.span: Vendor may appoint outside hosting, support, and operations providers after giving Customer a courtesy update. Vendor remains responsible for service availability.

deviations.original_text: Vendor may use subprocessors only with Customer's prior written approval or through an agreed notice-and-objection process. Vendor will bind each subprocessor to written obligations at least as protective as this DPA for Personal Data and remains responsible for each subprocessor's acts and omissions.
deviations.mutated_text: Vendor may appoint outside hosting, support, and operations providers after giving Customer a courtesy update. Vendor remains responsible for service availability.

distractors.span: Vendor will provide an annual SOC 2 Type II report, responses to reasonable security and privacy questionnaires, and other information reasonably necessary to demonstrate compliance with this DPA.
distractors.why_benign: The audit canary says an annual SOC 2 Type II report may satisfy routine assurance if follow-up rights remain available.

distractors.span: For up to 30 days after termination, Vendor will provide reasonable termination assistance to support secure transition, return, or deletion.
distractors.why_benign: The transition canary says a 30-day termination assistance period is acceptable.

distractors.span: If this DPA conflicts with the master services agreement regarding Personal Data, this DPA controls for that Processing.
distractors.why_benign: A DPA precedence clause for personal-data processing is customary and client-protective.
